The coins were old. Not just tarnished old, but a mint mark nobody in the settlement recognized.
Whoever hid it did not want it found quickly. The entrance was behind a false wall that only collapsed by accident.
The chest was locked but the lock was decorative. Solid metal casing with no keyhole. Whatever opened it was not a key.
Most of the contents had corroded beyond use. But the pieces that survived were finer than anything the local smiths produce.
A prospector found the first piece wedged between two stones in the creek bed. He spent a week looking for more and found nothing.
The cache was buried at a depth that suggests it was meant to be retrieved. Someone planned to come back and never did.
There were markings on the container that matched symbols found on the old ruins to the north. Same hand, maybe.
The gemstones were uncut but sorted by color and size. Whoever left them knew their value and took care with the arrangement.
A child found it while playing in a collapsed cellar. Brought it home thinking it was a toy. The parents realized what it was worth.
Three groups have tried to reach the lower chamber and all three turned back. The passage narrows to a crawl and the air gets thin.
The map is genuine as far as anyone can tell, but the landmarks it references do not match the current terrain. Either the land changed or the map is very old.
It was not gold or silver. Some kind of alloy that does not tarnish and holds an edge without sharpening. Nobody knows what to call it.
Whoever left it set traps that still function. Simple mechanisms, but effective. One of the scouts lost a boot to a spring-loaded plate.
The haul was split among the finders, as is custom. But one of them insists there was more in the vault that the others took before the count.
We sent a sample to a trader who deals in antiquities. He went quiet for a long time before offering three times what we asked. That made us suspicious.
The location was marked on an old survey chart that had been filed and forgotten. Nobody thought to check the archive until now.
There is almost certainly more down there. The first chamber was only partially cleared and the passage continues deeper than anyone has gone.
